sections.title: AI Agent (MCP)
sections.description: Operate Daily Dose from any MCP-compatible AI agent — submit standups, review your team, and run admin actions without opening Slack.

subsections.title: Use Daily Dose from your AI agent

content.title: 🤖 What is this?
content.value: Daily Dose exposes a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server, so you can run your standups straight from an AI assistant like Claude Desktop or Cursor. Connect once — just add the server and sign in with Slack — then ask your agent in plain language; it talks to Daily Dose for you.
**What you can do**

items: Submit or update your standup for any team
Review your own recent standup history
Team admins & owners: view a team's standup, post summaries, and send reminders

content.title: 🔐 Tied to your Slack identity
content.value: Every action runs as you. Your connection is only your identity — it never lets your AI agent do anything you couldn't already do yourself in Slack. Admin-only tools still require you to be a team admin or owner, checked on every call.

**Just add the server URL — that's it.** Daily Dose signs you in automatically (OAuth). In any client that supports remote MCP servers — Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code Copilot, and others — add Daily Dose by its URL and nothing else. The first time you use it, your client opens a Slack login in your browser; approve it and you're connected. There's no token to generate, copy, or paste.

# MCP server URL
https://dd.jnahian.me/mcp
**Claude Desktop** — add Daily Dose as a remote MCP server by URL; it prompts you to sign in with Slack on first use.

content.title: 🔄 Connected once, managed anytime
content.value: Your AI client keeps its access fresh automatically — you won't be asked to sign in again unless you disconnect. See and disconnect your connected AI clients anytime from the token page: https://dd.jnahian.me/mcp-tokens

subsections.title: Step 2 — Install in your coding agent

content.value: Pick your client below. Every one connects to the same server URL and signs you in with Slack the first time — there's no token to copy. If your client isn't listed (or doesn't support OAuth sign-in), use the manual-token method in the next section.

# MCP server URL
https://dd.jnahian.me/mcp
**Claude Code (CLI)** — add the server, then run `/mcp` inside Claude Code and approve the Slack sign-in in your browser. Add `--scope user` to make it available in every project.

claude mcp add --transport http daily-dose https://dd.jnahian.me/mcp

# then, inside Claude Code:
/mcp
**Claude Desktop** — open **Settings → Connectors → Add custom connector**, paste the server URL, and save. Click the new connector to sign in with Slack.
**Cursor** — add this to `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (global) or `.cursor/mcp.json` (this project). Cursor opens a browser sign-in on first use and remembers it.
**VS Code (GitHub Copilot)** — add this to `.vscode/mcp.json` in your workspace. The root key is `servers` (not `mcpServers`), and you need Copilot **Agent mode** to use the tools.
**Windsurf** — add this to `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`, then refresh MCP servers in Cascade. Windsurf uses `serverUrl` (not `url`).

content.title: 🔐 First-use sign-in
content.value: Whichever client you choose, the first request opens a Slack login in your browser. Approve it once and your client keeps the connection fresh automatically — no token to manage. Review or disconnect clients anytime at https://dd.jnahian.me/mcp-tokens

subsections.title: Advanced — connect with a manual token

content.value: You only need this if your AI client **doesn't** support automatic OAuth sign-in. Instead of adding the server by URL alone, you generate a personal access token and send it in an `Authorization` header.
**1. Generate a token.** Open the token page, sign in with Slack, and click **Generate Token** (optionally name it like “Cursor” or “Claude Desktop”).

# Token page
https://dd.jnahian.me/mcp-tokens

content.title: Shown only once
content.value: The full token (it starts with `ddm_`) is displayed a single time, right after you generate it. Copy it now and store it like a password — it can't be retrieved again. Lost it? Just generate a new one and revoke the old. Tokens expire 90 days after creation and can be revoked anytime from the same page.
**2. Add the server with your token** in an `Authorization: Bearer` header. For Claude Desktop that looks like:
These tools are available to every team member. Because your AI agent has no Slack channel context, name the team explicitly — or ask it to list your teams first.

content.description: List the teams you belong to, with your role.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Which Daily Dose teams am I on?

content.description: Submit today's standup for a team. Provide any of: last working day's tasks, today's tasks, blockers (at least one).
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Submit my Engineering standup — yesterday I shipped the auth fix, today I'm doing code review, no blockers.

content.description: Submit or update your standup for a specific date (YYYY-MM-DD), today or in the past.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Update my Engineering standup for 2026-06-15 — today's tasks should say “released v1.14”.

content.description: Show your own standup submissions. Defaults to the last 7 days.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Show my standups from last week.

subsections.title: Let your agent draft your standup

content.title: ✍️ Draft from your other tools
content.value: If your AI agent is also connected to your work tools (GitHub, Jira, ClickUp, Trello), it can gather what you did and draft your standup for you — then show you a preview and submit only after you confirm. Daily Dose never connects to those tools itself; it relies on the connections your agent already has.

**Just ask, and your agent pulls the details for you.** It reads your recent activity from whatever tools it's connected to — git commits and PRs, Jira, ClickUp, Trello — works out what you did since your last working day and what's on deck today, then drafts the standup. In clients that support prompts (like Claude Desktop), run the **compose_standup** prompt as a one-step shortcut.
**Example prompts** — your agent fetches the work itself:

items: *“Draft my Engineering standup from my git commits and open PRs, then show me a preview.”*
*“Pull my in-progress Jira tickets and build today's standup — preview it before submitting.”*
*“Check my ClickUp tasks and GitHub activity, draft my standup, and ask me to confirm.”*
*“Summarize the Trello cards I moved yesterday into my standup and let me review it first.”*

content.description: Render a standup draft for a team without saving it. Shows exactly what will be submitted, whether it will overwrite an existing entry, and whether it counts as late — so you can review before confirming.
examples:
# Your agent gathers your work from its connected tools, drafts the
# three fields, calls preview_standup, then shows you the result and asks:
Here's your Engineering standup drafted from your GitHub + Jira activity. Submit it?

content.title: You always confirm
content.value: Your agent is instructed to preview and get your explicit yes before submitting, and never to invent work you didn't do. Nothing is written to Daily Dose until you approve it.

subsections.title: What you can do — team admins & owners

content.title: Requires admin or owner
content.value: These tools act on a whole team — viewing everyone's responses, posting to the channel, or DMing members. Daily Dose checks that you're an admin or owner of that team on every call.

content.description: View a team's standup for a date: all responses (on-time and late), who hasn't submitted, and who's on leave.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Show today's Engineering standup — who responded and who's missing?

content.description: View one member's standup submission for a date. Identify them by Slack id, name, or username.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
What did Bob submit for Engineering yesterday?

content.description: Post a team's standup summary for a date to its channel. Refuses if there are no responses yet.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Post the Engineering standup summary to the channel.

content.description: Post one member's standup as a threaded reply (creates the team thread if needed).
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Post Alice's standup to the Engineering thread.

content.description: DM today's standup reminder to active team members who haven't opted out.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Send standup reminders to the Engineering team.

content.description: DM a follow-up reminder to team members who still haven't submitted.
examples:
# Ask your agent:
Send a follow-up to Engineering members who haven't submitted yet.

items: **Identity-scoped** — your token only does what you can already do in Slack
**Admin tools are gated** — viewing, posting, and reminders require team admin/owner, re-checked on every call
**Revoke anytime** — kill a token instantly from the token page; all tokens expire after 90 days
**Name your team** — tools take a team name (or id); if unsure, ask your agent to list your teams first

content.title: “Your Slack account isn't registered”
content.value: You'll see this when signing in if you're not yet a member of any team in Daily Dose. Join a team in Slack first (your workspace admin can add you), then sign in again.

content.title: “Invalid or expired token” (401)
content.value: Your token was revoked or has passed its 90-day expiry. Generate a fresh token on the token page and update it in your AI client's config.
